abstract: Comprehensive React 19/19.2 best-practices guide for AI agents and LLMs. Contains 44 rules across 8 categories, prioritized by impact from critical (concurrent rendering, server components) to incremental (component patterns). Covers React 19 headline changes (ref as a regular prop, native document metadata, resource preload APIs, useActionState, useOptimistic, use() hook, Context as provider) and 19.2 features (Activity, useEffectEvent, cacheSignal, React Compiler v1.0). Each rule includes detailed explanations, real-world examples comparing incorrect vs. correct implementations, and specific impact metrics to guide automated refactoring and code generation.
